author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John xvi. 24, ‘Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name;’ and on the words of Luke x. 17, ‘Lord, even the demons are subject unto us in thy name.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John xvi. 24, ‘Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name;’ and on the words of Luke x. 17, ‘Lord, even the demons are subject unto us in thy name.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Holy Gospel was being read, we heard what in truth ought at once to put every earnest soul in motion to seek, not to faint.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whoso is not moved, is not changed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there is a dangerous movement, of which it is written, “Suffer not my feet to be moved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there is another movement of him who seeketh, knocketh, asketh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then has been read we have all heard; but I suppose we have not all understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It makes mention of that which together with me ye should seek, with me ask, for the receiving of which ye should with me knock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as I hope the grace of the Lord will be with us, that whereas I wish to minister to you, I too may be thought worthy to receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it, I pray you, that we have just heard that the Lord said to His disciples?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hitherto have ye asked nothing in My Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is He not speaking to those disciples, who, after He had sent them, having given them power to preach the Gospel, and to do mighty works, returned with joy, and said to Him, “Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through Thy Name”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye recognise, ye recollect this which I have quoted from the Gospel, which in every passage and every sentence speaketh truth, nowhere false, nowhere deceiveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then is it true, “Hitherto have ye asked nothing in My Name”? and, “Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through Thy name”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of a surety this puts the mind in motion to ascertain the secret of this difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore ask we, seek, knock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be there in us faithful godliness, not a restlessness of the flesh, but a submission of the mind, that He who seeth us knocking may open unto us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the Lord then may give to be ministered unto you, do ye with earnest attention, that is, with hunger, receive; and when I shall have spoken it, ye will doubtless with sound taste approve what is placed before you out of the Lord’s store.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus knew whereby the soul of man, that is, the rational mind, made after the image of God, could be satisfied: only, that is, by Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This He knew, and knew that it was as yet without that fulness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knew that He was manifest, and He knew that He was hidden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knew what in Him was exhibited, what concealed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knew all this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How great,” says the Psalm, “is the multitude of Thy sweetness, O Lord, which Thou hast hidden to them that fear Thee; which Thou hast wrought for them that hope in Thee!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy sweetness” both great and manifold “hast Thou hidden to them that fear Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hidest it to them that fear Thee, to whom dost Thou open it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hast wrought it for them that hope in Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A twofold question has arisen, but either is solved by the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If any one inquires after the other, what is this, “Thou hast hidden it to them that fear Thee; wrought it for them that hope in Thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are they that fear, and they that hope, different?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not the very same who fear God, hope in God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who hopeth on Him who doth not fear Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who in a godly sort feareth Him, and hath not hope in Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let this then first be solved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Somewhat would I say concerning those who hope and those who fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law hath fear, Grace hope.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what difference is there between the Law and Grace, since the Giver both of the Law and Grace is One?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law alarmeth him who relieth on himself, Grace assisteth him who trusteth in God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law, I say, alarmeth; do not make light of this because it is brief; weigh it well, and it is considerable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look well at what I have said, take what we minister, prove wherefrom we take it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law alarmeth him who relieth on himself, Grace assisteth him who trusteth in God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What saith the Law?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many things: and who can enumerate them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I bring forward one small and short precept from it which the Apostle hath brought forward, a very small one; let us see who is sufficient for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the Law; if there be no grace, thou hast heard thy punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou boast to me whosoever thou art that hearing this dost rely upon thyself, why dost thou boast to me of innocence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou flatter thyself thereupon?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst say, “I have not plundered the goods of others;” I hear, I believe, perhaps I even see it, thou dost not plunder the goods of others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, “Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I do not go in to another man’s wife;” this again I hear, believe, see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, “Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou inspect thyself all round without, and dost not inspect within?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look in, and thou wilt see another law in thy members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look in, why dost thou pass over thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Descend into thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt “see another law in thy members resisting the law of thy mind, and bringing thee into captivity in the law of sin which is in thy members.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason then is the sweetness of God hidden to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The law placed in thy members, resisting the law of thy mind, bringeth thee into captivity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of that sweetness which to thee is hidden, the holy Angels drink; thou canst not drink and taste that sweetness, captive as thou art.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hadst not known concupiscence, unless the Law had said, Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou heardest, fearedst, didst try to fight, couldest not overcome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “sin taking occasion by the commandment wrought death.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely ye recognise them, they are the Apostle’s words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Sin taking occasion by the commandment, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why didst thou vaunt thyself in thy pride?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, with thine own arms hath the enemy conquered thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou verily, didst look for a commandment as a defence: and, lo, by the commandment the enemy hath found an occasion of entering in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For sin taking occasion by the commandment,” he saith, “deceived me, and by it slew me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means what I said, “With thine own arms hath the enemy conquered thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the same Apostle going on, and saying; “Wherefore the Law indeed is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make answer now to the revilers of the Law: make answer on the Apostle’s authority, “The commandment is holy, the Law holy, the commandment just and good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was then that which is good, made death unto me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But sin that it might appear sin, by that which is good wrought death in me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is this but because on receiving the commandment thou didst fear, not love?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou fearedst punishment, thou didst not love righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso feareth punishment, wisheth, if it were possible, to do what pleaseth him, and not to have what he feareth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbiddeth adultery, thou hast coveted another’s wife, thou dost not go in unto her, thou dost not do so, opportunity is given thee, thou hast time, a favourable place is open, witnesses are absent, yet thou dost not do it, wherefore?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because thou fearest the punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But no one will know it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will not God know it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So it is clear, because God knoweth what thou art about to do, thou doest it not; but here thou fearest the threatenings of God, not lovest His commandments.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou not do it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because if thou do, thou wilt be cast into hell fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the fire thou fearest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O if thou didst love chastity, thou wouldest not do it, even though thou mightest be altogether unpunished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If God were to say to thee, “Lo, do it, I will not condemn thee, I will not condemn thee to hell fire, but I will withhold My Face from thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou did it not because of this threat, it would be from the love of God that thou didst not do it, not from the fear of judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wouldest do it, perhaps I mean thou wouldest do so; for it is not my place to judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou do it not on this principle because thou abhorrest the contamination of adultery, because thou lovest His precepts, that thou mayest obtain His promises, and not because thou fearest His condemnation, it is the grace which maketh saints that aideth thee; it is all of grace, ascribe it not to thine own self, attribute it not to thine own strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou actest from delight in it, well; thou actest in charity, well; I assent, I agree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Charity worketh by thee, when thou actest with thy will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At once dost thou taste sweetness, if thou hope on the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whence hast thou this charity, if yet thou hast it? for I am afraid lest even yet it is through fear thou doest it not, and lest thou seem great in thine own eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if it is through charity that thou doest it not, thou art truly great.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have,” you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “From myself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Far art thou from sweetness, if thou hast it from thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt love thine own self, because thou wilt love that from which thou hast it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I will convict thee that thou hast it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in that thou dost think that thou hast so great a thing from thine own self, by that very fact I do not believe thou hast it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou hadst, thou wouldest know from whence thou hadst it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou charity from thyself, as if it were some light, some little thing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If thou shouldest speak with the tongues of men and Angels, but have not charity, thou wouldest be a sounding brass and a tinkling cymbal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou shouldest know all mysteries, and have all knowledge, and all prophecy, and all faith so that thou couldest remove mountains, but not have charity,” these things could not profit thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If thou shouldest distribute all thy goods to the poor, and deliver up thy body to be burned, but not have charity, thou wouldest be nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How great is this charity, which if it be wanting, all things profit nothing!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Compare it not to thy faith, not to thy knowledge, not to thy gift of tongues, to lesser things, to the eye of thy body, the hand, the foot, the belly, to any one lowest member compare charity, are these least things to be in any way compared to charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the eye and nose thou hast from God, and hast thou charity from thine own self?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hast given thyself charity which surpasseth all things, thou hast made God of light account with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more can God give thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatever He may have given, is less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Charity which thou hast given thyself, surpasseth all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou hast it, thou hast not given it to thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what hast thou which thou hast not received?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who gave to me, who gave to thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge Him in His gifts, that thou feel not His condemnation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By believing the Scriptures, God hath given thee charity, a great boon, charity, which surpasseth all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God gave it thee, “because the charity of God hath been shed abroad in our hearts;” by thine own self, perhaps?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid; “by the Holy Ghost, who hath been given us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Return with me to that captive, return with me to my proposition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Law alarmeth him that relieth on himself, grace assisteth him who trusteth in God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For look at that captive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He seeth another law in his members resisting the law of his mind, and leading him captive in the law of sin, which is in his members.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, he is bound, lo, he is dragged along, lo, he is led captive, lo, he is subjected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath that profited him, “Thou shalt not lust”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath heard, “Thou shalt not lust;” that he might know his enemy, not that he might overcome him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For he had not known concupiscence,” that is, his enemy, “unless the Law had said, Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou hast seen the enemy, fight, deliver thyself, make good thy liberty, let the suggestions of pleasure be kept down, unlawful delight be utterly destroyed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Arm thyself, thou hast the Law, march on, conquer if thou canst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what good is it that through the little portion of God’s grace thou hast already, thou “delightest in the Law of God after the inward man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou seest another law in thy members resisting the law of thy mind;” not “resisting” yet powerless for aught, but “leading thee captive in the law of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, whence to thee who fearest that “plentifulness of sweetness is hidden!” to him that feareth it “is hidden,” how is it” wrought” out for him that “trusteth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cry out under thine enemy, for that thou hast an assailant, thou hast an Helper too, who looketh upon thee as thou fightest, who helpeth thee in difficulty; but only if He find thee “trusting;” for the proud He hateth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then wilt thou cry under this enemy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Wretched man that I am!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see it already, for ye have cried out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be this your cry, when haply thou art distressed under the enemy, say ye, in your inmost heart say, in sound faith say, “Wretched man that I am!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wretched that I am!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Therefore wretched,” because “I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Wretched man that I am,” both because “I,” and because “man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “he is disquieted in vain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For though “man walketh in the Image;” yet, “wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wilt thou thyself? where is thy strength, where is thy confidence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of a surety thou both criest out, and art silent; silent, that is, from extolling thyself, not from calling upon God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be silent, and cry out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God Himself too is both silent, and crieth aloud; He is silent from judgment, He is not silent from precept; so be thou too silent from elation, not from invocation; lest God say to thee, “I have been silent, shall I be silent always?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cry out therefore, “O wretched man that I am!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge thyself conquered, put thine own strength to shame, and say, “Wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did I say above?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law alarmeth him that relieth upon himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, man relied upon himself, he attempted to fight, he could not get the better, he was conquered, prostrated, subjugated, led captive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He learnt to rely upon God, and it remaineth that him whom the Law alarmed while he relied upon himself, grace should assist now that he trusteth in God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this confidence he saith, “Who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The grace of God by Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now see the sweetness, taste it, relish it; hear the Psalm, “Taste and see that the Lord is sweet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath become sweet to thee, for that He hath delivered thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast bitter to thine own self, when thou didst rely upon thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Drink sweetness, receive the earnest of so great abundance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The disciples then of the Lord Jesus Christ while yet under the Law had to be cleansed still, to be nourished still, to be corrected still, to be directed still.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they still had concupiscence; whereas the Law saith, “Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Without offence to those holy rams, the leaders of the flock, without offence to them I would say it, for I say the truth: the Gospel relates, that they contended which of them should be the greatest, and whilst the Lord was yet on earth, they were agitated by a dissension about pre-eminence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence was this, but from the old leaven? whence, but from the law in the members, resisting the law of the mind?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They sought for eminence; yea, they desired it; they thought which should be the greatest; therefore is their pride put to shame by a little child.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jesus calleth unto him the age of humility to tame the swelling desire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason then when they returned too, and said, “Lord, behold even the devils are subject unto us through Thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: (It was for a nothing that they rejoiced; of what importance was it compared to that which God promised?)

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord, the Good Master, quieting fear, and building up a firm support, said to them, “In this rejoice not that the devils are subject unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because “many will come in My Name, saying, Behold, in Thy Name we have cast out devils; and I will say to them, I know you not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this rejoice not, but rejoice because your names are written in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye cannot yet be there, yet notwithstanding ye are already written there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “rejoice.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So that place again, “Hitherto have ye asked nothing in My Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what ye have asked, in comparison with that which I am willing to give, is nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what have ye asked in My Name?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the devils should be subject unto you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In this rejoice not,” that is, what ye have asked is nothing; for if it were anything, He would bid them rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then it was not absolutely nothing, but that it was little in comparison of that greatness of God’s rewards.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Apostle Paul was not really not anything; and yet in comparison of God, “Neither is he that planteth anything, neither he that watereth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so I say to you, and I say to myself, both to myself and you I say, when we ask in Christ’s Name for these temporal things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye have asked undoubtedly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who doth not ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One asketh for health, if he is sick; another asketh for deliverance, if he is in prison; another asketh for the port, if he is tossed about at sea; another asketh for victory, if he is in conflict with an enemy; and in the Name of Christ he asketh all, and what he asketh is nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then must be asked for?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ask in My Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He said not what, but by the very words we understand what we ought to ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask, and ye shall receive, in My Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not nothing; but what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That your joy may be full;” that is, ask what may suffice you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when thou askest for temporal things, thou askest for nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whoso shall drink of this water, shall thirst again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He letteth down the watering pot of desire into the well, he taketh up whereof to drink, only that he may thirst again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ask, that your joy may be full;” that is, that ye may be satisfied, not feel delight only for a time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask what may suffice you; speak Philip’s language, “Lord, show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord saith to you, “Have I been so long time with you, and have ye not known Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Philip, he that seeth Me, seeth the Father also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Render then thanks to Christ, made weak for you that are weak, and make ready your desires for Christ’s Divinity, to be satisfied therewith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn we to the Lord, etc.
